en-US.CREDITS_BODY:
<color=#FFFFFF66>UI based on Overlayer (modlist.org)</color>
<color=#FFFFFF88>Thank you for using Quartz.</color>
<size=12><color=#FFFFFF33>
Licensed under GPLv3</color></size>
en-US.DESC_ACCENT_COLOR: Recolors the whole Quartz UI. Middle-click to reset.
en-US.DESC_AD_ON: Deafens you on Discord once a run passes the percent below, and undeafens on death/finish. Needs your own Discord application: paste its Client ID, then Authorize.
en-US.DESC_DL_MESSAGE: Shown on the fail screen when a limit kills the run.
en-US.DESC_EDITOR_BGA_HIDE_PLANET_DECO: Also hides decorations attached to a planet while BGA Mod is hiding the level. Background and camera-anchored decorations are left visible.
en-US.DESC_EDITOR_BGA_HIDE_TILE_DECO: Also hides decorations attached to tiles while BGA Mod is hiding the level. Background and camera-anchored decorations are left visible.
en-US.DESC_EDITOR_BGA_MOD: Hides every tile and planet while the level is playing, so only the background shows — for recording a background animation (BGA) to composite gameplay over. The editor's edit view is unaffected; hiding only kicks in during play-test or actual gameplay.
en-US.DESC_EDITOR_HORIZONTAL_PROPERTIES: Lays each level-editor inspector property out as "label [field]" on one row, instead of the label stacked above the field. Affects the in-game editor, not this settings window.
en-US.DESC_EDITOR_RENDER_RESOLUTION: Output video resolution. Pick a preset or fine-tune with the width/height sliders below. The frame is captured at this size regardless of the game window.
en-US.DESC_EDITOR_RENDER_SAMPLE: Render only the first few seconds of the level instead of the whole thing — handy for checking audio sync and settings on long levels.
en-US.DESC_EDITOR_RENDER_SPEED: How fast the output plays relative to real time. 1.00x is the level exactly as played; lower is slow-motion, higher is sped up. Audio is stretched or compressed to match.
en-US.DESC_EDITOR_RENDER_LEADIN: Seconds the planet spins silently to warm up before recording starts — this is NOT in the video. An auto render skips the count-in and begins on the frame the level loads, while the pipeline is still priming, which can leave the whole clip out of sync. The warm-up absorbs that hitch, then recording begins clean at the song. 0 turns it off.
en-US.DESC_EDITOR_RENDER_PREROLL: Seconds the video holds on the opening still — with silent audio — before the song starts. Unlike the warm-up spin, this IS in the output: the clip opens on a freeze of the start, then plays, so it doesn't cut straight onto the first note. 0 turns it off.
en-US.DESC_EDITOR_RENDER_FPS: Output frame rate. The engine is locked to this rate during the render, so every frame is captured — heavy levels render slower than real time but never drop frames.
en-US.DESC_EDITOR_RENDER_BITRATE: Target video bitrate in megabits per second. Higher is better quality and larger files. 16 Mbps is a good default for 1080p60.
en-US.DESC_EDITOR_RENDER_CODEC: Video encoder. x264 is the most compatible; the hardware encoder (macOS) is fastest; x265 makes smaller files but encodes slower; AV1 (SVT-AV1) makes the smallest files but is the slowest to encode.
en-US.DESC_EDITOR_RENDER_AUDIO: Records the full game mix — music and hit sounds — in sync with the video. Turn off for a silent capture.
en-US.DESC_EDITOR_RENDER_FLIP: Flips the captured frame top-to-bottom. On by default because Unity reads the screen bottom-up; turn off only if your recordings come out upside down.
en-US.DESC_EDITOR_RENDER_OUTPUT: Folder the .mp4 files are written to. Leave blank to use UserData/Quartz/Renders. Each render is named with a timestamp.
en-US.DESC_EDITOR_RENDER_BUTTON: Arms the renderer. Play a level and the whole run is recorded offline, frame by frame, then saved as an mp4 when you reach the end. The level is locked while rendering (auto-play on); a white screen shows the frame progress. Press Esc to cancel.
en-US.DESC_EDITOR_SHOW_FLOOR_ANGLE: Shows the total angle (in degrees) of the selected tiles, on one of the tiles in the level editor. Affects the in-game editor, not this settings window.
en-US.DESC_EDITOR_SHOW_FLOOR_BEATS: Shows the selected tiles' length in beats (total angle ÷ 180), on one of the tiles in the level editor. Affects the in-game editor, not this settings window.
en-US.DESC_EDITOR_SHOW_FLOOR_COUNT: Shows how many tiles are selected, on one of the tiles in the level editor. Affects the in-game editor, not this settings window.
en-US.DESC_EDITOR_SHOW_FLOOR_DURATION: Shows how long the selected tiles take to play, in seconds, on one of the tiles in the level editor. Affects the in-game editor, not this settings window.
en-US.DESC_EDITOR_USE_TULTTAK_BEHAVIOR: Matches the Tulttak mod's behaviour by leaving the last selected tile out of the angle, beats, and duration totals.
en-US.DESC_DL_ON: Counts misses and overloads during a run (useful with No Fail) and fails the run once a cap below is exceeded.
en-US.DESC_FXRM_ON: Strips the selected effect events from charts as they load. Reload the level to apply changes.
en-US.DESC_FXRM_REMOVE_ALL_DECO: Off keeps decorations that judgement-conditional events reference (hit/miss feedback) and removes the rest.
en-US.DESC_FXRM_SAVE: While the remover is on, the editor holds the stripped copy of the level — saving would overwrite the original chart, so it's blocked unless you enable this.
en-US.DESC_FXRM_TUTORIAL_PATTERNS: Also hides the default background's tiled pattern. Its pulsing shapes are always removed while Background is on.
en-US.DESC_JPOP_ON: Suppresses the selected judgement popups — the text that pops up at the planet on every hit.
en-US.DESC_JR_MESSAGE: Shown on the fail screen when the restriction kills the run.
en-US.DESC_JR_ON: Instantly fails the run when a hit breaks the chosen rule.
en-US.DESC_JUDGEMENT_ENABLED: Top-center row of hit counts per judgement, from Overload through Perfect to Miss.
en-US.DESC_JUDGEMENT_XPERFECT: Split the Perfect count into +Perfect / X / -Perfect when the XPerfect mod is active.
en-US.DESC_KCB_ON: Drops repeats of the same key arriving within the threshold — chattering switches fire multiple times per press. Blocked presses are logged.
en-US.DESC_KEYVIEWER_DM_CSS_IMPORT: Select a DM Note custom CSS file. Layers over the preset; restyles keys and counters.
en-US.DESC_KEYVIEWER_DM_IMPORT: Select a DM Note preset JSON file.
en-US.DESC_KEYVIEWER_ENABLED: On-screen key grid with per-key press counters, KPS and total presses.
en-US.DESC_KEYVIEWER_KEYLABEL: Custom caption for the selected key. Leave empty to derive it from the bound key.
en-US.DESC_KEYVIEWER_RAIN: Streaks rising from a key while it's held.
en-US.DESC_KEYVIEWER_RAINWIDTH: Streak width for the front key row. 0 matches each key's width.
en-US.DESC_KEYVIEWER_RESETCOUNTS: Clears every per-key press counter and the total.
en-US.DESC_KEYVIEWER_COUNTFORMAT: Show counters with a thousands separator (1,234).
en-US.DESC_KEYVIEWER_PERKEYKPS: Each key box shows that key's presses-per-second instead of its total press count.
en-US.DESC_KEYVIEWER_HIDEMAINCOUNT: Hide the per-key counters on the key boxes.
en-US.DESC_KEYVIEWER_STREAMER: Hide the KPS and Total stat boxes entirely.
en-US.DESC_KEYVIEWER_PERKEYCOLORS: Give each key its own colours instead of the shared ones above.
en-US.DESC_KEYVIEWER_PERKEYFONT: Give each key its own font sizes instead of the shared ones above.
en-US.DESC_KEYVIEWER_PK_COPYGLOBAL: Overwrite every key's per-key colours and font sizes with the current shared values.
en-US.DESC_KEYVIEWER_PK_COPYTHIS: Copy this key's colours onto every key and turn on separate colours for all of them.
en-US.DESC_KEYVIEWER_PK_COLORSENABLE: Give just this key its own colours. The other keys keep the shared colours until you turn theirs on too.
en-US.DESC_KEYVIEWER_PK_FONTENABLE: Give just this key its own font sizes. The other keys keep the shared sizes until you turn theirs on too.
en-US.DESC_KEYVIEWER_GHOST: Show a separate ghost-coloured streak while a key's secondary (ghost) key is held. Doesn't change the counters.
en-US.DESC_KEYVIEWER_FOOTSTYLE: Add a separate foot-pedal element you can drag on its own in Reorganize mode. The keys light on press but don't count.
en-US.DESC_KEYVIEWER_SHOWOUTSIDE: Keep the key viewer visible in menus and outside of gameplay, not just while a level is playing.
en-US.DESC_KEYVIEWER_SYNCLIMITER: Overwrites the Key Limiter's allowed keys with the keys shown here, and keeps them matched when you rebind keys or switch styles.
en-US.DESC_KL_CAPTURE: Press any key to add/remove it from the allowed list. Escape cancels.
en-US.DESC_KL_ON: Only the allowed keys below count as gameplay hits while playing. Mouse buttons are always allowed; menus and the editor are unaffected.
en-US.DESC_MIDDLE_CLICK_TO_SET_AS_DEFAULT:
Setting that restores an item to its default value when you middle-click on it.
You can identify it by a small dot at the top-left of the item
en-US.DESC_OTTO_HIGHBPM_ON: On: Otto uses the color below while the level's top BPM is 300+ (where vanilla turns him red). Off: the normal color is always used.
en-US.DESC_OTTO_ON: Replaces the editor's Otto (auto-play) icon with the mod's icon, tinted with the color below. Dimmed automatically while auto-play is off.
en-US.DESC_OVERLAY_ENABLED: Master switch for every overlay HUD — panels, progress bar, combo and judgement.
en-US.DESC_PANEL_ADDSTAT: Pick a stat to add to this panel.
en-US.DESC_PANEL_BGCOLOR: Fill color of the panel background. The A (alpha) slider sets its opacity. Needs Background Panel on.
en-US.DESC_PANEL_LAYER: Drag to reorder. Panels higher in the list draw on top where they overlap.
en-US.DESC_PANEL_LOCALIZESTATS: Off: this panel's stat labels stay English (X-Acc, Max X-Acc…). On: they follow the UI language.
en-US.DESC_PANEL_NAME: Shown on the panel while reorganizing, and as this section's title.
en-US.DESC_PANEL_PREFIX: Extra line shown at the top of the panel.
en-US.DESC_PANEL_STATCOLOR_MAXBPM: BPM that maps to the 100% end of the gradient.
en-US.DESC_PANEL_STATCOLOR_ON: Tints this stat's value by blending the colors below across the stat's own 0–100% range.
en-US.DESC_PANEL_STATCOLOR_PERFECT: Overrides the gradient with this color while the stat sits at exactly 100% — v1's gold accuracy.
en-US.DESC_PANELS_CREATE: Adds a new empty panel. Name it, put stats on it, then drag it into place with Reorganize.
en-US.DESC_PCOL_ON: Recolors the planets (and their tails) with the colors below, overriding level events and skins.
en-US.DESC_PROFILE_ADD: Creates a new profile from your current settings and switches to it.
en-US.DESC_PROFILE_IMPORT: Loads a .qprofile (or legacy .krprofile) file as a new profile. It won't be selected automatically.
en-US.DESC_PROFILE_RECALIBRATE: Re-baseline overlay positions to this monitor, keeping them exactly where they are now. Positions then scale proportionally if the profile is used on a different-sized display.
en-US.DESC_PROGRESSBAR_PREFILLSTART: When a run starts mid-chart (checkpoint or editor play), the bar starts already filled up to that point instead of starting empty.
en-US.DESC_PROGRESSBAR_FILLGRADIENT: Shift the fill colour as the run progresses (0% to 100%) instead of using one flat colour.
en-US.DESC_PCOL_SEP_TAIL: Off: tails use the ball color (with their own opacity). On: each planet's tail gets its own color.
en-US.DESC_PCOL_RING: Paint the planet ring a custom colour. When off, the ring is hidden while planet colours are active.
en-US.DESC_TW_BCP: Removes the planets' core and spark particles.
en-US.DESC_TW_CP: Strips checkpoint icons and behavior from the level — dying always restarts the run. Turning this off needs a level reload to bring icons back.
en-US.DESC_TW_GLOW: Suppresses the glow flash tiles get when the planet lands on them.
en-US.DESC_TW_MENUBPM: Sets the menu rabbit's two speeds to the BPMs below instead of the default 1x / 2x. Re-open the menu to apply.
en-US.DESC_TW_MENUMUSIC: Mutes the theme song on the title and island-select screens. Takes effect immediately; gameplay music is untouched.
en-US.DESC_TW_NOPAUSE: While auto-play is on, the game pauses itself (e.g. when the window loses focus). This blocks those automatic pauses — pausing manually still works.
en-US.DESC_TW_PGLOW: Hides the glow sprite drawn around the planets.
en-US.DESC_TW_RESULT_ACC: Removes the Accuracy row from the detailed results screen.
en-US.DESC_TW_RESULT_CHECKPOINTS: Removes the Checkpoints Used row from the detailed results screen.
en-US.DESC_TW_RESULT_MAXKEYS: Removes the Maximum Used Keys row from the detailed results screen.
en-US.DESC_TW_RESULT_XACC: Removes the X-Accuracy row from the detailed results screen.
en-US.DESC_TW_SCROLL: Ignores mouse wheel input while a level is being played, so accidental scrolling can't affect the game mid-run.
en-US.DESC_UIH_ON: Hides the selected pieces of the game's own UI. Two profiles: Playing and Recording — the shortcut below flips between them mid-game.
en-US.DESC_UIH_RECMODE: Which profile is live right now: off = Playing, on = Recording.
en-US.DESC_UPDATE_CHANNEL: Which builds to receive when updating. Alpha includes every build; each step up is more stable, with Stable being only final releases.
en-US.DESC_UPDATE_NOTES: Opens this release's notes on GitHub.
en-US.DESC_UPDATE_SKIP: Hides this version. You'll still be offered the next release.
en-US.RENDER_STATUS_ARMED: Armed — play a level to start rendering
en-US.EDITOR_USE_TULTTAK_BEHAVIOR: Prevent the last tile from being counted in timing calculation
en-US.USE_FONT_IN_GAME_OVERLAY: Use font in the in-game overlay
en-US.DESC_USE_FONT_IN_GAME_OVERLAY: Apply the selected font to A Dance of Fire and Ice's own in-game overlay (the level title and artist shown during play), not just this mod's UI. The default SUIT font keeps the game's own font.
en-US.DESC_SETTINGS_FONT: Font for this mod's own settings window. "Same as overlay font" follows the Font picker above.
en-US.FXRM_RESET_OPACITY: Reset All Track Opacity Values to 100%
en-US.JR_MESSAGE_HINT: Use {judgement} for the judgement you broke.
en-US.KEYVIEWER_KEYS_HINT: Click a key to open its editor.
en-US.KEYVIEWER_PERKEY_HINT: Click a key in the preview to edit it. These switch per-key colours/fonts on.
en-US.KEYVIEWER_PK_COPYTHIS: Copy This Key's Colors to All Keys
en-US.KEYVIEWER_PK_FONTENABLE: Separate Font Sizes For This Key
en-US.DESC_KEYVIEWER_PK_REBIND: Click, then press the new key for this slot. Esc cancels.
en-US.DESC_KEYVIEWER_GHOSTSET: Click, then press the secondary (ghost) key for this slot. Esc cancels.
en-US.KEYVIEWER_GHOST_NONE: Select a key above to set its ghost key.
en-US.KEYVIEWER_FOOT_HINT: The foot keys are a separate element — drag them anywhere in Reorganize mode.
en-US.KL_SYNC_LOCKED: Keys are managed by the Key Viewer (Sync Keys to Key Limiter is on).
en-US.ONLY_AVAILABLE_WHEN_ENABLED: Only available when the Mod is Enabled!
en-US.DESC_OPT_SMOOTHGC: Holds off garbage collection while a level is playing and runs it when the run ends, so a GC pause can't land mid-run and nudge your timing. The heap grows during the run (a safety collect kicks in on very long levels). Best paired with Clean Heap On Load.
en-US.DESC_OPT_COLLECTONLOAD: Runs a garbage collection every time a scene loads, so each run starts from a clean heap. The load screen already hitches, so the collection is free here.
en-US.DESC_OPT_PRIORITY: Asks the OS to give the game more consistent CPU time (Above Normal priority). Takes effect on Windows; ignored where the system doesn't allow it (usually macOS/Linux).
en-US.DESC_OPT_RUNINBG: Keeps the game running at full speed when its window loses focus, so a run or practice session doesn't stall when you alt-tab.
en-US.DESC_OPT_LOSSYTEXTURE: Compresses custom textures loaded from disk (DXT) to cut their memory use ~4-8x, with a small visual quality cost. Applies to textures loaded after it's turned on.
en-US.PANEL_ALL_STATS_ADDED: All stats are already on this panel.
en-US.PROFILE_HINT: Every setting lives in the active profile. Export a profile before updating the mod manually, then import it back if your UserData gets replaced.
en-US.PROFILE_STATUS_IMPORT_FAILED: Import failed — not a Quartz profile file.
en-US.SEARCH_HINT: Type to search every tab — categories, toggles, buttons, everything.
en-US.UPDATE_FAILED_NETWORK: Couldn't reach GitHub — check your connection.
en-US.UPDATE_FAILED_NOT_FOUND: Update check failed — release feed not found.
en-US.UPDATE_FAILED_RATE_LIMIT: GitHub rate limit reached — try again later.
en-US.UPDATE_INSTALLED: Update installed — restart the game to apply.
en-US.UPDATE_SKIPPED: Skipped {0} — it won't be offered again.
en-US.IMPORT_HINT: Pull your settings in from another ADOFAI mod. Quartz reads each supported mod loaded through Unity Mod Manager and copies over what it has a home for. Your other settings are left untouched.
en-US.IMPORT_NONE: No supported mods detected. Load one through Unity Mod Manager — KorenResourcePack (v1), JipperResourcePack, JipperKeyViewer, ADOFAI Tweaks, KeyboardChatterBlocker, or Enhanced Effect Remover — then press Rescan.
en-US.DESC_IMPORT_DO: Copy this mod's settings into Quartz. Settings it doesn't cover are left as they are.
en-US.DESC_IMPORT_PROFILE: Copy this mod's settings into a new Quartz profile, leaving the current profile selected.
en-US.DESC_IMPORT_RESCAN: Re-scan for supported mods loaded through Unity Mod Manager.
en-US.DESC_NOST_NORESULT: Blanks the congratulations/results text shown when you finish a level.
en-US.DESC_NOST_HIDEDIFFICULTY: Hides the difficulty stars/selector and forces Strict, like the old game.
en-US.DESC_NOST_HIDENOFAIL: Hides the editor No-Fail button and prevents No-Fail from being turned on.
en-US.DESC_NOST_OLDPRACTICE: Press P on the fail screen to drop straight into practice mode.
en-US.DESC_NOST_SPEEDCHANGE: Shows a rabbit/snail icon on tiles whose speed changed even slightly.
en-US.DESC_NOST_SPEEDCHANGE_DETAIL: Only show the icon when the change exceeds the minimum below.
en-US.DESC_NOST_SPEEDCHANGE_MIN: Relative speed difference under which a tile counts as same-speed.
en-US.DESC_NOST_LEGACYFLASH: Re-fires the old full-screen flash when you take a hit.
en-US.DESC_NOST_NOJUDGEANIM: Snaps the hit-judgement text into place instead of the pop animation.
en-US.DESC_NOST_LATEJUDGE: Shows the judgement text on the previous tile, the old way.
en-US.DESC_NOST_LEGACYTWIRL: Draws spin tiles with the old swirl-and-arrow sprite.
en-US.DESC_NOST_TWIRLNOARROW: Use the legacy swirl but omit the direction arrow.
en-US.DESC_NOST_SPACE360: Pressing Space on a single selected tile adds a 360 spin tile.
en-US.DESC_NOST_WEAKAUTO: Restores the old, weaker auto-play behaviour.
en-US.DESC_NOST_WHITEAUTO: Keeps the auto indicator white on high-BPM levels.
en-US.DESC_NOST_EDITORBTNPOS: Moves the Auto / No-Fail / Difficulty buttons to their old positions.
en-US.DESC_NOST_EDITORBTNDESIGN: Gives the editor buttons the old outline-instead-of-shadow styling.
en-US.DESC_NOST_LEGACYTEXTS: Reverts a few Korean editor strings to their old wording. Korean only.
en-US.DESC_NOST_SFX_PUREPERFECT: Mutes the chime played on a pure-perfect hit.
en-US.DESC_NOST_SFX_DEATH: Mutes the death/explosion sound on a fail.
en-US.DESC_NOST_SFX_COUNTDOWN: Mutes the pre-level countdown takeoff sound.
en-US.DESC_NOST_SFX_ENDING: Mutes the ending cymbal at the end of a level.
en-US.DESC_NOST_SFX_NEWBEST: Plays the normal explosion instead of the new-best jingle.
en-US.DESC_NOST_LEGACYCLS: Restores the old custom-level-select search bar and keyboard shortcuts.
en-US.DESC_NOST_ALPHAWARNING: Skips the alpha-build warning splash on startup.
en-US.DESC_NOST_ANNOUNCE: Hides the announcement/news sign in the lobby.
en-US.DESC_NOST_OLDBG: Replaces the lobby background with the old one.
en-US.DESC_NOST_OLDBG_B: Choose the second old-background variant (off = A, on = B).
en-US.DESC_NOST_OLDXO: Adds the hidden 'XO?' world to the level select. Re-enter the level select to see it.
en-US.DESC_FXRM_S_FILTER: Turns off VFX filters (Grayscale, Arcade, etc.) at runtime without changing the chart.
en-US.DESC_FXRM_S_HOM: Skips the Hall of Mirrors effect.
en-US.DESC_FXRM_S_MOVEMAX: Caps how many tiles a single Move Track event can move. The maximum value means unlimited.
en-US.DESC_OPT_FASTBLOOM: Forces ADOFAI's bloom post-process to use its cheaper low-quality render path while bloom is active. This targets real GPU work and can improve FPS on bloom-heavy levels, with softer/less precise bloom.
en-US.DESC_OPT_SKIPNOOPFILTERS: Skips ADOFAI full-screen screen-tile/screen-scroll shader passes when their current values are visually identity, replacing the shader pass with a plain copy. This removes real render work without wrapping an existing game setting.
